programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On Mississippi's Gulf Coast, a judge and his politician wife were brutally murdered in their home. The killer left few clues and the investigation led nowhere. But the FBI was patiently waiting in the wings. Once it was determined that federal laws had been violated, they pounced on the investigation. They uncovered a corrupt local government and a group of violent criminal conspirators known as the Dixie Mafia, responsible for fraud, money laundering and murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On February 3, 1990, a 14-year-old girl ran away from her mother's home and disappeared without a trace. Her mother hoped Pasadena, Texas police could find her before it was too late. Six months later, their worst fear was realized when a farmer discovered the young girl's remains in an Illinois barn. The FBI was called in to help. Agents tracked down a trucker who they believed was responsible for 50 more deaths. Investigators knew they had to stop this menace of the highways.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Two couples found themselves on the same deserted island paradise. But only one couple left alive. Buck Walker and Stephanie Stearns arrived in Hawaii on the Sea Wind, which belonged to the missing couple, Mac and Muff Graham. Buck and Stephanie were arrested and served time for boat theft. But years later, bones were discovered on the deserted island and identified as the remains of Muff Graham.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Beginning in California in 1995, a serial killer targeted vulnerable women. At first charming and generous, he quickly became violent. He was brutal with his kills and quick in his getaways. Traveling across the country, he struck in Mississippi, Louisiana and Florida. The FBI and local police needed to stop this cross-country killer before more women fell victim to his insatiable rage.

programme.title: Los archivos del FBI (Doblado)
programme.desc: El primero de octubre de 1993, un extraño secuestró a Polly Klaas de doce años durante una piyamada en su casa. El FBI de inmediato entró en el caso. Los agentes reunieron fibras de su habitación y descubrieron, a través del uso de fuentes de luz láser, la huella de una palma en la cama de Polly, previamente invisible al ojo humano. Después de una extensa investigación de dos meses, el FBI arrestó a Richard Allen Davis. La impresión de la palma y las fibras coincidieron y Davis fue condenado por secuestro y asesinato.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1992, a young mother was gunned down in front of her children. Police searched for answers, but found none. The horrible crime appeared unmotivated, senseless, and random. Then, family members offered a startling clue. The victim, the wife of a prominent Atlanta attorney, was also a woman burdened with a secret; a secret the FBI believed led to her death. It would force local and federal agents to untangle an intricate web of money, drugs and murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Beginning in summer of 1990, the bodies of several women began turning up in and around the city of Vienna, Austria. From an extensive investigation by the Viennese police, it was determined that these murders were the work of one sadistic predator. Europe was dealing with a serial killer. With no experience with this kind of predator, Viennese police turned to the FBI. FBI profilers were able to identify a suspect in the murders. More ominously, they linked this same killer to murders committed in the United States.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the late 80's, Florida's cocaine imports were among the highest in the nation. Regional territories were enforced with intimidation and murder. Desperate to stop the violence, local authorities turned to the FBI for help. One dealer bowed to no one. He would stop at nothing to keep detectives and agents from building a case against him. When his organization began targeting the government itself, a city went on high alert.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: California Highway Patrolman George Gwaltney discovered the body of 23-year-old Robin Bishop near her car on a remote desert highway. Although he proclaimed his innocence the FBI lab experts were able to link tool marks found on the frame with tools found in Gwaltney's home. Through the match of hairs and fibers and a unique test for detecting and identifying sperm, Gwaltney was placed at the scene of the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Memphis, Tennessee, a horrible crime terrified local residents. When a wealthy young mother was abducted in front of her in-laws' home, the police had no shortage of suspects. The twenty-five year old victim left behind a tumultuous marriage, an ex-husband, and several spurned boyfriends. All were on the FBI's short list. To find the woman and her abductor, investigators would have to first determine the motive.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On Mississippi's Gulf Coast, a judge and his politician wife were brutally murdered in their home. The killer left few clues and the investigation led nowhere. But the FBI was patiently waiting in the wings. Once it was determined that federal laws had been violated, they pounced on the investigation. They uncovered a corrupt local government and a group of violent criminal conspirators known as the Dixie Mafia, responsible for fraud, money laundering and murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a series of brutal bank robberies and murders in south Miami during the mid-1980's, the FBI embarked on an exhaustive eight-month investigation. With little evidence and no suspects, agents were willing to try anything to find the violent perpetrators. On April 11, 1986, agents conducting routine surveillance spotted the vehicle used in the bank robberies. They attempted to arrest the two suspects in the car. What followed was the bloodiest firefight in FBI history.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Larry Horn nearly got away with the perfect crime: a multiple murder-for-hire plot that was meticulously planned and executed. Horn's former wife, their 8-year-old son, and a live-in nurse were all brutally murdered by an assassin. In the absence of any significant clues, police in Maryland contacted the FBI to assist in the investigation.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A stranger snatched an eight-year-old girl as she played in her Tucson, Arizona neighborhood, leaving behind an abandoned bicycle and a frantic mother. Hundreds of citizens and police officers fanned out across the region, hoping to find some trace of the young girl or her abductor. Within hours, a suspect emerged; a drifter who was out on parole for abducting and molesting children. He was nowhere to be found and time had run out for the innocent child.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Kokomo, Indiana an unthinkable crime terrified residents. A young woman was snatched from her home on a bright spring morning. Drops of blood and a torn window screen were all that was left to tell authorities what happened to the twenty-one year old victim. After several days of searching, a former FBI profiler narrowed the suspect list to one. Agents and detectives followed the trail across state lines hoping to find the victim before her time ran out.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1980, the FBI began a massive undercover operation, code named Safebet, that was designed to expose the Chicago Mafia's infiltration into the city's surrounding suburbs. Undercover agents quickly discovered that the mob had its tentacles spread into every facet of the law enforcement community. They unwittingly uncovered the murder of a wealthy socialite perpetrated by a jealous and greedy mob lawyer and covered up by the local police chief.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A cunning predator was loose in rural South Carolinaabducting young women from their own front yards in broad daylight. He was also a sadist, calling one victim's family, taunting them, boosting and then dashing their hopes. Local investigators called on the FBI to help them find this clever serial killer. Like a game of chess, FBI profilers and forensic examiners were able to outwit the killer, and put an end to his deadly game of cat and mouse.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Langley, Virginia, a gunman fired into rush hour traffic. The brazen daytime shooting of employees from the Central Intelligence Agency stunned the country. The victims seemed randomly selected, but the motive appeared to be political. The FBI was called in, but the nationwide search quickly became a far-reaching international manhunt. Agents processed more than 2000 leads and crossed an ocean in the effort to deliver justice.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the mid-1990's, a gang of professional bank robbers raided cities across seven states. Disguises and speed protected their identity from cameras and the police as they stole hundreds of thousands of dollars, always leaving a bomb to terrorize their victims. The stakes were raised when the FBI discovered the robbers had an agenda beyond their personal wealth. To stop these extremists and their growing operation, the FBI needed to capture their leaders.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In November of 1994, when a Wells Fargo armored van disappeared from a Glendale, Arizona mall, local authorities and the FBI scrambled to recover it, and its cargo of nearly a million dollars. Six hours later, agents found the van, its driver shot dead inside and the money gone. It was a perfect hit by professionals who left no clue behind. Months passed before a solid lead developed. In January, a tip led agents to look into a former prison guard.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The rapes and murders of ten women near Tampa, Florida in 1984 had the authorities puzzled and the community scared. Since the beginning, the FBI had been analyzing the crime scene evidence and had found a common link: red trilobal carpet fibers. But there was no suspect, until one of the killer's potential victims survived. Through her interviews and the cooperation of local authorities and the FBI, 31-year-old Bobby Joe Long emerged as the prime suspect.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 90s, Washington, D.C. police were losing the battle against street gangs who enforced their territories with lethal violence. The homicide rate in the nation's capital climbed to the highest in the country. Local police turned to the FBI for help, but agents soon found themselves in the direct line of fire. In the fall of 1994, a gang member walked into police headquarters, killed two FBI agents and one detective, then turned the gun on himself.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A serial killer's rampage was discovered when the bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves that dotted the Alaskan wilderness. The killer taunted his victims, hunting them like animals before shooting them down. He was at home in the rugged terrain. But the hunt for him began 3000 miles away, where FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: He was a recluse who spread terror through the mail for 18 years. Two men died and more than a dozen were injured, but Ted Kaczynski wasn't known as the Unabomber until his brother tipped off the FBI. Kaczynski's “Unabomber Manifesto,” published in major newspapers, set off a flurry of activity for the FBI: writing analysis and comparisons, DNA gleaned from a single postage stamp and finally, Kaczynski's arrest at his tiny cabin.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In rural Texas, 13-year-old is kidnapped on her way to school. Frantic to find her safely, her millionaire father calls local authorities and the FBI. As police officers and agents conduct aerial surveillance, the father drives hundreds of miles through the night to deliver the ransom cash to a drop that the kidnappers keep changing. In a final effort to save the young girl, investigators must engage in a dangerous firefight with five desperate criminals.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A 1981 armored car robbery near New York City left one guard dead and another critically wounded. Police stopped suspects nearby and another shoot-out ensued, claiming the lives of two officers. Local police and the FBI suspected a large group of domestic terrorists with a history of violent crime. They would try to ferret out these heavily armed criminals who moved in a shadowy underworld and were not afraid to kill authorities.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: He was a recluse who spread terror through the mail for 18 years. Two men died and more than a dozen were injured, but Ted Kaczynski wasn't known as the Unabomber until his brother tipped off the FBI. Kaczynski's “Unabomber Manifesto,” published in major newspapers, set off a flurry of activity for the FBI: writing analysis and comparisons, DNA gleaned from a single postage stamp and finally, Kaczynski's arrest at his tiny cabin.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On Mississippi's Gulf Coast, a judge and his politician wife were brutally murdered in their home. The killer left few clues and the investigation led nowhere. But the FBI was patiently waiting in the wings. Once it was determined that federal laws had been violated, they pounced on the investigation. They uncovered a corrupt local government and a group of violent criminal conspirators known as the Dixie Mafia, responsible for fraud, money laundering and murder.

programme.title: Los archivos del FBI (Doblado)
programme.desc: El 21 de junio de 1964, tres defensores de los derechos civiles, uno blanco y uno negro, desaparecieron de Mississippi mientras cruzaban el condado de Neshoba. Una investigación del FBI a gran escala llevó a la recuperación de los cuerpos de los hombres asesinados y la evidencia física fue recuperada y analizada. Los laboratorios del FBI confirmaron que todas excepto una de las balas usadas para asesinar a los hombres provenían de la misma arma. El FBI se ganó la confianza de un informante, cuya información condujo a la condena de siete miembros del Ku Klux Klan local.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Two couples found themselves on the same deserted island paradise. But only one couple left alive. Buck Walker and Stephanie Stearns arrived in Hawaii on the Sea Wind, which belonged to the missing couple, Mac and Muff Graham. Buck and Stephanie were arrested and served time for boat theft. But years later, bones were discovered on the deserted island and identified as the remains of Muff Graham.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In February of 1996, New Jersey millionaire Frank Black flew to Florida to close a major business deal before retiring. He never came home. Authorities in New Jersey sensed foul play and brought in the FBI. Agents learned of a mystery woman in Florida who was to meet Black. Phone records showed a flood of calls to Black from his business rival just before the disappearance.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A day that began with a class field trip ended with a parent's worst nightmare. An 11-year-old girl was missing. Her parents were certain she was no runaway. When they failed to find her in their quiet Illinois neighborhood, they called the police. The FBI soon joined the investigation and family and friends worked with detectives and agents in an urgent search for the missing girl. Faced with conflicting witness reports and false leads, agents wouldn't stop until they found the girl, and the truth.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the mid-1990's, a gang of professional bank robbers raided cities across seven states. Disguises and speed protected their identity from cameras and the police as they stole hundreds of thousands of dollars, always leaving a bomb to terrorize their victims. The stakes were raised when the FBI discovered the robbers had an agenda beyond their personal wealth. To stop these extremists and their growing operation, the FBI needed to capture their leaders.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Langley, Virginia, a gunman fired into rush hour traffic. The brazen daytime shooting of employees from the Central Intelligence Agency stunned the country. The victims seemed randomly selected, but the motive appeared to be political. The FBI was called in, but the nationwide search quickly became a far-reaching international manhunt. Agents processed more than 2000 leads and crossed an ocean in the effort to deliver justice.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On August 20, 1980, an unseen sniper murdered two young black men as they jogged with two white women in Salt Lake City, Utah. Police threw all their resources into finding a killer who left almost no clues behind. They met with FBI agents and detectives from around the country who had similar racially motivated sniper slayings. A month later, working off little more than a possible license plate number, police in Kentucky arrested Joseph Paul Franklin but the man escaped from custody.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a summer afternoon in upscale Lodi, California, a 12-year-old girl is abducted from her home. Scores of police officers and FBI agents converge, determined to find the young girl before it is too late. When the kidnapper's car is discovered in a field near Lodi, police dogs, helicopters, and hundreds of volunteers are employed in a round-the-clock search. It would take a community united in concern for one of their youngest to find the missing child.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When five-year-old Melissa Brannen disappeared from a Christmas party in December 1989, the only probable suspect was 24-year-old Caleb Hughes. Hughes reportedly left the party around the time Melissa vanished, but no one saw him with the girl. Fifty blue acrylic fibers collected during a search of Hughes' car, however, became the major piece of evidence in the case. FBI fiber analysis determined that those fibers were identical to the ones that made up the sweater Melissa was wearing.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Kokomo, Indiana an unthinkable crime terrified residents. A young woman was snatched from her home on a bright spring morning. Drops of blood and a torn window screen were all that was left to tell authorities what happened to the twenty-one year old victim. After several days of searching, a former FBI profiler narrowed the suspect list to one. Agents and detectives followed the trail across state lines hoping to find the victim before her time ran out.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Arizona, a shipment of money vanished into the high desert, along with the two men hired to guard it. The FBI didn't know if the guards were participants or victims of an ambush. Answers lay somewhere in the thousands of square miles of Arizona's northwest territory. When the vehicle was recovered with three hundred thousand dollars and the drivers missing, suspicions that the crime was an inside job grew.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, a series of crimes in a small Iowa farming community left residents reeling in shock and fear. Two young men went on a crime spree of robbery, kidnapping and murder, and then disappeared. By the time they were identified, they already had a twelve-hour lead. As the hunt for the two men expanded, the FBI committed its resources to tracking down the suspects. The pursuit would take agents across six states to catch the killers before they struck again.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In June of 1992, masked bandits pulled off a daring mid-day bank robbery. It marked the beginning of a four-year crime wave that rocked Seattle. With each new robbery, the bandits perfected their craft, leaving behind no evidence and escaping with hundreds and thousands of dollars. As the robbers became increasingly violent, pressure mounted to end their reign. With few leads, the FBI forced their own luck. The effort put them face to face with an armed and desperate fugitive.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a California man was found dismembered in 1990, police suspected a local burglar, but they couldn't find him. Six months later, he was caught robbing an Arizona bank and sentenced to life. But prison couldn't hold him for long, and within a year he escaped. The FBI would lead a multi-agency manhunt for a killer on a desperate run through the tourist regions of the west.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On June 21, 1964, three civil rights workers, two white and one black disappeared in Mississippi while passing through Neshoba County. A massive FBI investigation led to the recovery of the bodies of the slain men and physical evidence was recovered and analyzed. The FBI labs confirmed that all but one of the bullets used to kill the men came from the same gun. The FBI gained the trust of an informant, whose information led to the conviction of seven local Ku Klux Klan members.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On February 26, 1993, an explosion rocked the World Trade Center parking garage. Six people died and more than a thousand were injured in the bomb blast that tore a 5-story crater in the building. During the investigation, the twisted fender of a rental van was extracted from the rubble. The FBI was able to determine from where the van was rented and by whom. Undercover agents then made their arrests within six days of the bombing.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a teenage immigrant came to the United States to realize her dream of becoming a doctor. But a tragic twist of fate intervened, when she found herself an innocent victim of the drug trade. Kidnapped and held captive, she survived for days against all odds. Authorities arrested her captors, but they refused to reveal her whereabouts. The FBI and local police raced against time, determined to save the teenager's life. Their investigation revealed a complex story of drugs, betrayal and revenge.

programme.desc: Wedded couples vow to stay together, until death do they part. This series looks at what happens when a bride or groom causes their beloved’s demise.

programme.title: Dateline NBC

programme.title: Dateline NBC

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The decade-old murder of a young Texas housewife is investigated.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a hit-and-run boating accident caused the death of a popular young man, investigators faced the daunting task of searching for one boat among 1200 others.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial killer was on the loose and police had to find him before he struck again. Their most promising lead was an unusual one: a bloody fingerprint on the body of one of the victims.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, construction manager Darrell North was found dead in his work trailer in Ft. Worth, Texas. When investigators arrived, they found that he had been stabbed 46 times, indicating a personal relationship between the victim and the attacker.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On May 20, 2001, Carrie Nelson, then 20, was beaten to death while working alone in a park office. The case went unsolved for six years until May 2007 when police found a match with DNA samples gathered from the crime scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The bomb was constructed to cause as much damage as possible... and it did, killing the victim with deadly force and flame. A painstaking search yielded tiny clues, which identified the killer as surely as if he'd signed them.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2007, the Florida mobile home of Effie and Michael Ratley catches fire and Michael heroically rescues his wife and infant son. A month later, his wife is found beaten to death in a bedroom of his parents' home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim had ingested a massive amount of cyanide. An unlikely clue _ a flaw on a mailing envelope _ exposed a murderer who was willing to kill innocent people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2004, Rebecca Barney and her soon-to-be ex-husband Fred were found shot to death in their Tulsa, Oklahoma, home, which had been set on fire.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The prime suspect had a criminal record, and his driver's license was found at the scene of a brutal double-homicide.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The woman could have lost control of her SUV on the icy road, and plunged down an embankment into a shallow creek. But there were footprints in the snow leading away.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A successful stockbroker told his wife he'd be working late, and when he didn't come home, she reported him missing.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Michigan State University grad student disappeared and was presumed dead. With the help of a professor of geological sciences, police hoped to get the 'dirt' on her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After shooting his victims in the head, the killer staged the scene, placed the incriminating evidence into a plastic bag and tossed it into the river.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Xploration Animal Science
programme.desc: In this episode we'll see the amazing strength of the leaf cutter ant. Find out how fast can a pronghorn really run, and how the desert camel is able to survive in such extreme conditions.

programme.title: Xploration Animal Science
programme.desc: The Siberian Tiger is the biggest cat in the World. It takes down large prey with its 5 inch long canine teeth. And the American badgers sharp front claws make it one of the best burrowers. Plus toads undergo one of the most amazing transformations!

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet
programme.desc: This week we see how nature can sometimes be the best classroom. Philippe heads to Key Largo to join a high school class getting up close with local sea grass habitats. Then we head to the streams of Pennsylvania to go on a 'Bio-Blitz' surveying aquatic insects. From there it's up to the Pacific Northwest to see how volunteers use camera traps to keep tabs on elusive animals.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet
programme.desc: This week we look at our microscopic world. Philippe heads to the University of Texas to break down the microscopic world of sand. He'll stay on campus to then look at how microscopic science plays a role in researching fire ants. From there it's off to the University of Pennsylvania to get deeper look into soil. And finally researchers at USC study some of the smallest organisms in the ocean.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet
programme.desc: This week we take flight within the animal kingdom. Philippe joins a researcher to go into Congaree National Park in South Carolina to catch and tag local bats. We then look at the flight patterns and tendencies of wood storks. And finally from caterpillar to butterfly we examine the transformation from crawling to flying.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet
programme.desc: In this episode, Cousteau takes us to the American mid-West to see how wildlife in Yellowstone National Park cope with the harsh winter environment. We then climb to the top of Zion National Park’s most famous peak before heading to a Pacific island.

programme.title: Hombres hay muchos
programme.desc: Raquel descubre que su marido es infiel y viaja a Cancún para atraparlo. Con la ayuda de sus dos amigos tratará de sabotear sus vacaciones, pero termina encontrando el amor.

programme.title: El justiciero
programme.desc: Santino y Tavor, dos peligrosos explotadores sexuales con una amplia red de trata de blancas han secuestrado a una chica que llegó a su organización a base de engaños. Roberto hará todo lo posible por rescatarla sin importar lo que tenga que hacer.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Adaptación de uno de los libros más reconocidos de la Biblia, que aborda la historia del origen de la humanidad, invitando a entender el presente, volviendo al principio de todo. La caída de Lucifer, la vida de Adán y Eva, Noé y el diluvio, y otros.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: Maria do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: La trayectoria de Jesucristo antes de la resurrección. Sus milagros y el calvario de la cruz. Las vidas de quienes fueron curados por Jesucristo y quiénes eran los apóstoles.

programme.title: El Laberinto de Alicia
programme.desc: Gregorio saca a la luz la oscura verdad que nunca quiso recordar; Sofía toma el control sobre el colegio.

programme.title: La tierra prometida
programme.desc: Historia bíblica que sigue a Josué mientras lidera a los hebreos hacia Canaán. Josué enfrenta diversos desafíos, incluyendo la oposición de Samara, quien está enamorada de él.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Geraldo no pierde oportunidad de coquetear con Malvina. Leoncio le roba un beso a Isaura. La próxima horca de Coqueiro tiene a la sociedad consternada. Álvaro tuvo que salir de emergencia.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Leoncio le asegura a Isaura que Álvaro la ha dejado para siempre. Leoncio planea mandar una carta para que Isaura se decepcione. Belchior le hace una propuesta a Isaura para que el plan de boda continúe.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Geraldo desaprueba las acciones de Blanca. Leoncio piensa usar a Miguel para que Isaura se case con Belchior. Sebastián coquetea con Gioconda luego de que éste soñara con ella.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Paulo forza a Helena a volver a casa y amenaza con hacerla suya. Leoncio le muestra a Miguel la carta falsa y le ofrece un acuerdo. Álvaro se reúne con Perpetua.

programme.desc: In the 1960s, Americans were dying at shocking rates due to inadequate emergency care. Enter Freedom House Ambulance Service: the first paramedics unit in the U.S., run and staffed by Black men and women. This is the origin story of EMS.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Americans are obsessed with coffee and Nicole Estaphan is brewing up some good stories. She explores the history of coffee in Boston (was it coffee, not tea, that fueled the Revolution?) and meets the coffee trailblazer who founded the late, great Coffee Connection. Nicole learns latte art from the expert baristas at Ogawa in Boston, and if you love beer and coffee, at Marlborough “brew” house Lost Shoe you don’t have to choose! Finally, Nicole talks to a doctor who champions (moderate) coffee consumption.

She explores the history of coffee in Boston (was it coffee, not tea, that fueled the Revolution?) and meets the trailblazer who founded the late, great Coffee Connection. programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A local man who claims to have the world’s largest collection of Crocs (shoes, not reptiles). A Montreal man turned Bruins fanatic whose home is bursting with black-and-gold memorabilia. And a photographer who collects… sunsets (we’ll explain). Tonight, we meet New Englanders who just can’t resist the temptation to collect… well, something. They include a local couple making a star turn on their own home renovation show; they invite Anthony Everett into the massive collection of antiques in their barn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It influences our moods, our buying habits, and can even determine how hungry we are. We are talking about color! Nicole Estaphan takes us on a journey through the rainbow as we learn how these wavelengths impact our feelings. She takes us to MIT, where a team is working with fabric that can change color whenever you want. From “Tyrion Purple” to “Oregon Blue” - Nicole stops at the Harvard Museum’s Forbes Pigment Collection to learn from some of the oldest varieties on the planet. Finally, have a love for all those holiday light displays? Nicole takes us to the Marshfield Fairgrounds to see how color ties everything together.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Anthony Everett and Shayna Seymour host an hour packed with gems from Chronicle’s vault, in honor of our 40th anniversary.

programme.title: WCVB 50 Years Special
programme.desc: (2022) WCVB Channel 5 celebrates 50 years - from the very first moments on the air to meeting the people who make it all happen.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Next time someone gives you a hard time about your pandemic diet (or lack thereof), ignore them and Flip the Bird...as in the Beverly restaurant, Flip the Bird, known for its variety of chicken sandwiches. Perhaps you’re a lobster lover or you prefer a plate of pasta for dinner? We’re trying them all tonight, along with spicy hot ramen, savory pies, and to wash it all down, hot Irish coffee.

programme.title: Third Coast
programme.desc: Who knew you could explore endless outdoor adventure, a vibrant food and wine scene, crystal blue freshwater, world-class golf, and a charming downtown- all in Northern Michigan? Join host Hannah Cumler as she showcases the best of Traverse City!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Boston’s best hot chocolate? Some say it’s at Cacao in Boston’s South End, so we check it out tonight. As the temperatures drop we are headed out in search of meals that warm our bellies.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Danny’s a family man now and needs to upgrade his smile, damaged by past drug use. But he’s been burned by dentists before. Tattoo artist Craig claims that no one’s been able to give him teeth he won’t break. Will their faith in dentists be restored?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Who says you have to cross state lines to find beautiful Main Streets and Back Roads? After all, there’s plenty to enjoy in our own backyard. Tonight, Anthony Everett heads to Concord, Massachusetts, a town with a rich concentration of history, culture, and natural beauty.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: People who say the Nutmeg State is boring just aren’t looking hard enough. This quiet corner of New England is home to a medieval castle (and its eccentric ruler), and the prized collection of the late “Nut Lady.”

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: You don’t need your own beach to throw your own clambake! Tonight Shayna Seymour gets advice from local chefs and restaurateurs on prepping summer specialties: Backyard burgers from Bred Gourmet, steamers at Woodman’s of Essex, and super-fancy s’mores from Cambridge’s Urban Hearth. Shayna also meets a retired police chief who’s dishing up pizza in his “golden years.”

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Expand your 'at-home' menu with these Fall dishes from the Cooking at Home Fall Foods edition.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As the temperatures plunge it’s tempting to “eat in” so we’re back with more home-cooking options. Bar Mezzana Chef Colin Lynch gets us back to healthy eating habits with a cooking demo and some slow-roasted black cod. We meet the local woman behind a new app that offers farmers’ market products from the comfort of a shopper’s home. And we shop with a personal chef taking a new approach to meal prep – using what’s in your pantry and stretching your grocery dollars.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We still have some summer left to hold onto, and it’s definitely been a “scorch-ah”! We have some ideas if you need to “cool down” a bit. Ted Reinstein visits some of Vermont’s best swimming holes, while Anthony Everett strolls along the pier and sands of Old Orchard Beach in Maine. We sip wine slushies, healthy smoothies, and shaved ice cream (yes, it’s a thing) at local businesses. We also check out a local lake with the longest name on record, and hit a waterfall in a spot many don’t know is right nearby.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: New Hampshire’s Lakes Region is a renowned summer playground, but tonight we’re exploring the beauty of the surrounding “Cotton Valley” in springtime. We keep our eyes peeled for the traditional “ice out” and take in a model yacht race on Lake Winnipesaukee. We also meet the couple who saved a historic local home, transforming it into an inn recently named by Travel and Leisure as the number one resort hotel in the U.S. And at Pavilion, a restaurant led by Top Chef alum Chris Viaud, we sample scrumptious seasonal fare.

programme.title: Third Coast
programme.desc: There's nothing like Benzie County! Join host Hannah Cumler as she celebrates the powerful women behind a thriving family resort, the Michiganders running the Midwest’s first grain-to-glass distillery, and toasts this can't-miss destination.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: New England has lost 10,000 dairy farms in the last 50 years, but today about 1,000 local farms are still producing milk, butter, and cheese – and innovating along the way. Tonight, Ted Reinstein visits four family-owned farms, three here in Massachusetts and one in New Hampshire, each with its own approach to surviving in a challenging industry, preserving family legacies, adapting new technologies to mitigate climate impact – and yes, producing delicious dairy products (including ice cream!).

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Dams can be found on just about every river in the Bay State. Some are enormous and serve the needs of millions while others crumble away. Some are quite lovely and others... less so. In fact, most dams in Massachusetts serve no purpose anymore. All of this has Ted Reinstein asking, what is the dam story? He gets to the bottom of it tonight.

programme.desc: Erika Tarantal hand-feeds giraffes and Anthony Everett joins the National Guard for a day as we try a few different careers on for size. Meanwhile, Erika tests whether she has what it takes to become a Gloucester firefighter, a guest services rep at the Encore Resort’s five-star hotel, and a zookeeper at the Franklin Park Zoo.

programme.desc: The Asian food scene is booming in Greater Boston, with many local restaurants focusing on region-specific dishes. Anthony Everett samples Thai “tapas” at Ma Hani Yom in Brookline. We visit old friends at Burmese restaurant Yoma in Allston. The popular Instagrammer known as “Boston Food Bae” takes us to her favorite udon restaurant, Futago in Boston. And we indulge in Korean dish Bibimbap at food truck Bibim Box. Those stops and more on tonight’s food journey.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Greater Boston you can still enjoy “dinner and a show.” At a Plymouth drive-in, movie-goers can catch a flick outdoors while enjoying elevated fare (Wagyu sriracha hot dogs, anyone?). On a Cambridge food tour, the “show” is the history and culture of the neighborhood. In Boston’s Fenway neighborhood, we find dinner and a country music show. programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Here’s a suggestion for beating the dog days of summer – stay inside watching a show about actual dogs! We visit outdoor dining destinations in Boston and Salem where you can bring your four-legged friend (including one that serves dogs their meals on a take-home frisbee).

programme.title: FIXT

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s our birthday! Chronicle debuted on Jan. 25, 1982, and we’re inviting you to help us celebrate with a week of classic Chronicle shows – all leading up to a jam-packed, prime-time special! Tonight we flash back to 1987 when it was “Big Apple or Bust” for the Chronicle crew. Mary Richardson took to the skies, Peter Mehegan hit the highway, and Mike Barnicle boarded the train. Who hit New York City’s Plaza Hotel first? Come along for the ride and find out!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we go backstage at local theaters, each with its own rich history – each celebrating a major milestone. Also tonight, a sit-down with jazz legend Herbie Hancock ahead of his recent performance at the Boch Center Wang Theater.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Erika Tarantal loves children, so it is no surprise some of her favorite stories this year were stories about children: keeping kids engaged with learning, giving them access to healthy activities, and connecting children to the environment. Tonight, a rundown of Erika’s 2021 highlights from her “Five for Good” series.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s that time of year, when we look back at the stories we’ve shared with you throughout the year – and the generous people who have shared their stories with us. Erika Tarantal's favorites.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan brings us eye-catching, eye-popping, “eye candy!” She meets artist Howie Green, famed for his colorful “pop-up” pop art, and discovers shoes that will blow your mind (and benefit good causes). In Winsted, Conn. Nicole checks out a 5-story-high mural that took 22 years to complete. And for those who believe we “taste with our eyes,” Nicole visits Jonquils in Boston, where a Ukrainian-born pastry chef used 3-D technology to dream up modern desserts, and Confectionery Designs in Rehoboth, where the sweets are to-eye-for.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: You can’t eat these Tootsie Rolls or Necco wafers – they’re works of art by a bead artist whose work is featured at a local gallery. Tonight Erika Tarantal brings us beautiful things and the people who create them.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we test whether New Englanders can agree on what qualifies as acceptable etiquette, from “manspreading” on the T to lane-cutting on Storrow Drive. Tune in to find out which very opinionated New Englander we invite to weigh in as our celebrity judge.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.desc: It's Spooky Season! Pumpkin carving, ghost adventures, Fall drinks and more!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: In Dorchester, a community newspaper is going strong thanks to a proud family legacy. In the North End, a local entrepreneur has turned her family’s longtime neighborhood pharmacy into an Italian restaurant. And one Needham restaurateur is so proud of his mother’s cake recipe that it graces the menu of his restaurant. Tonight, stories of families who choose to work together, and are committed to keeping business “all in the family.”

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From “open barnyard” at a South Shore farm to art classes on Beacon Hill and the Dr. Seuss Museum in Springfield, tonight we explore local activities and sites that are family-friendly (but fun for everyone).

programme.title: Ed & Day in The 'Burgh

programme.desc: From a popular new pro volleyball team drawing huge crowds, to a former football pro who now coaches e-sports, these Nebraskan athletes show how to seize the moment.
